response: This function computes the length of the longest common subsequence (LCS) between two sequences `a` and `b` using bottom-up dynamic programming with an (m+1)×(n+1) table, where each cell `dp[i][j]` stores the LCS length of the prefixes `a[:i]` and `b[:j]`. It fills the table by either extending the diagonal value when characters match or taking the max of the top/left neighbors otherwise, returning `dp[m][n]` as the final answer.
